analysis_result: CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP in MPNST-G2 vs G1: NES = +1.31 (up in G2), FDR = 0.344 (direction agrees; magnitude smaller; not significant).
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: Supplementary Figure 6b from paper PDF shows enrichment plots for MPNST-G2 tumors with CYCLIN_D1_UP annotation 'FDR: < 0.0001, NES: 1.9488'. The model's analysis used bulk RNA-seq with n=12 MPNST samples (6 G1 + 6 G2, M1933 excluded).

B. COHORT DEFINITION: Reported = MPNST-G2 vs other samples (likely all non-G2 tumors in the full cohort of 49 RNA-seq samples, not just G1). Model = restricted to 12 MPNST samples (6 G1 + 6 G2), using G2 vs G1 contrast only.

C. VARIABLE CODING: Reported = CYCLIN_D1_UP (MSigDB C6 - CYCLIN_D1_UP.V1_UP, 188 genes, matches model). Ranking metric assumed similar (signal-to-noise or t-statistic).

D. COMPUTATION: Reported NES = 1.9488, FDR < 0.0001. Model NES = +1.31, FDR = 0.344.

E. RESULT COMPARISON:
- NES: 1.31 vs 1.9488 → absolute difference 0.64, relative difference 32.8% (exceeds ±10% concordance threshold)
- FDR: 0.344 vs <0.0001 → >3000-fold difference in statistical significance
- Direction agrees (both positive, up in G2) but model lacks statistical significance

F. ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION:
The most likely explanation is a cohort/contrast difference. In the paper's GSEA analyses (Fig 3c, Supp Fig 6), MPNST-G1 and MPNST-G2 enrichment plots are typically computed against the full cohort or a broader reference (e.g., G2 vs all other subgroups, or against premalignant/benign NFs), not solely G2 vs G1 on 12 samples. This yields a much larger effect size because the contrast is stronger, and narrower confidence intervals due to higher sample counts.

Additional contributing factors:
1. Sample size difference: 12 samples (model) vs potentially 49 (paper) reduces statistical power and widens FDR.
2. Ranking metric: Signal-to-noise with 6 vs 6 samples yields noisy gene rankings; paper may use moderated t-statistic (limma) or Wald statistic (DESeq2), producing cleaner rankings.
3. Permutation scheme: gseapy.prerank with 1000 permutations vs likely gene-set permutation in original with potentially more iterations; for n<7 per group, GSEA best practice is gene-set permutation (which gseapy.prerank uses), but underlying ranking metric matters.
4. Normalization: RNA-seq normalization method (TPM vs DESeq2 median-of-ratios vs VST) may affect gene-level rankings.

G. MAGNITUDE: MAJOR discrepancy on FDR (>3000x), MINOR-to-MAJOR on NES (32.8% relative).
proposed_fix: Expand the GSEA reference group to include all non-G2 samples in the RNA-seq cohort (likely n=49 total, so G2 vs 43 others), rather than restricting to G1 only. Alternatively, use the full 49-sample cohort with a moderated test statistic (limma voom t-statistic) as the ranking metric instead of signal-to-noise on 12 samples. The paper's methods section likely specifies the exact contrast; verifying 'MPNST-G2 tumors' means vs all others vs vs G1 is critical. Also verify whether published analysis used signalToNoise, t-test, or log2FC for ranking.
